title: Kubernetes Setup Guide
content:
Kubernetes Setup Guide

Overview: This guide covers the installation and configuration of Kubernetes clusters for production workloads.

Prerequisites

  • Ubuntu 22.04 LTS or RHEL 8+

  • Minimum 4 CPU cores, 16GB RAM per node

  • Network connectivity between all nodes

  • Root or sudo access

Installation Steps (kubeadm)

1. Disable Swap

2. Install Container Runtime (containerd)

3. Install Kubernetes Components

4. Initialize Control Plane (Master Node)

5. Install CNI (Calico)

Verification

Production Considerations

  • Use at least 3 control plane nodes for HA

  • Configure etcd backup (daily)

  • Enable audit logging

  • Set up RBAC policies

Related: Helm Charts Configuration, Container Registry Setup

title: Incident Response Procedures
content:
Incident Response Procedures

Purpose: This document outlines the standard operating procedures for handling production incidents.

Severity Levels

LevelDescriptionResponse TimeExamples
P1 - CriticalComplete service outage affecting all users15 minutesDatabase down, API gateway failure, security breach
P2 - HighMajor feature unavailable, significant user impact30 minutesPayment processing down, login failures for subset
P3 - MediumMinor feature degradation, workaround available4 hoursSlow response times, minor UI bugs
P4 - LowMinimal impact, cosmetic issuesNext business dayTypos, non-critical warnings

P1 Incident Workflow

  1. ALERT RECEIVED - PagerDuty notification triggered

  2. ACKNOWLEDGE - On-call engineer acknowledges within 5 minutes

  3. ASSESS - Determine scope and impact (5-10 minutes)

  4. COMMUNICATE - Post in #incident-war-room Slack channel

  5. ESCALATE - Page additional team members if needed

  6. MITIGATE - Apply immediate fix or rollback

  7. RESOLVE - Confirm service restoration

  8. POST-MORTEM - Schedule within 48 hours

Communication Templates

Initial Incident Message

Status Update

Rollback Procedures

See deployment runbooks for service-specific rollback commands:

  • Kubernetes deployments: Use Helm rollback

  • Database migrations: Contact DBA team

  • Feature flags: Disable via LaunchDarkly

Post-Incident

  • Complete incident timeline in PagerDuty

  • Create JIRA ticket for post-mortem

  • Schedule blameless post-mortem meeting

  • Document action items and owners

Owner: SRE Team

Escalation: sre-oncall@company.com

title: Monitoring & Alerting Setup
content:
Monitoring & Alerting Setup

Overview: This guide covers the installation and configuration of our monitoring stack: Prometheus for metrics collection and Grafana for visualization.

Architecture

Installing Prometheus Stack (Kubernetes)

Key Dashboards

  • Cluster Overview: Node health, pod status, resource utilization

  • Application Metrics: Request rate, error rate, latency (RED method)

  • Database Monitoring: Connection pools, query performance

  • Business Metrics: Orders/min, active users, revenue

Alert Rules Examples

0.1 for: 5m labels: severity: critical annotations: summary: "High error rate detected" - alert: HighLatency expr: histogram_quantile(0.95, rate(http_request_duration_seconds_bucket[5m])) > 2 for: 10m labels: severity: warning annotations: summary: "P95 latency above 2 seconds"]]>

Access: https://grafana.company.internal

Owner: Observability Team

  • Minimum 8GB RAM, 4 CPU cores

title: PostgreSQL Backup & Recovery
content:
PostgreSQL Backup & Recovery

Overview: Comprehensive guide for PostgreSQL backup strategies, recovery procedures, and disaster recovery testing.

Backup Strategy

MethodToolFrequencyUse Case
Logical Backuppg_dumpDailySingle database, schema changes
Physical Backuppg_basebackupDailyFull cluster, faster recovery
Continuous ArchivingWAL-GContinuousPoint-in-time recovery

pg_dump - Logical Backup

Full Database Backup

Schema Only

Specific Tables

pg_basebackup - Physical Backup

WAL-G Configuration

Install WAL-G

Configure Environment

export AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY= export AWS_REGION=us-east-1 export PGHOST=/var/run/postgresql export PGUSER=postgres]]>

Create Backup

Recovery Procedures

Restore from pg_dump

Point-in-Time Recovery (WAL-G)

> /var/lib/postgresql/16/main/postgresql.auto.conf touch /var/lib/postgresql/16/main/recovery.signal # Start PostgreSQL sudo systemctl start postgresql]]>

Disaster Recovery Testing

Schedule: Monthly DR drill (first Saturday)

Procedure:

  1. Restore latest backup to DR environment

  2. Verify data integrity with checksums

  3. Run application smoke tests

  4. Document recovery time

RTO: 4 hours | RPO: 1 hour

Owner: Database Team
